name: Anjini Jamwal
objective: Enthusiastic and dedicated Computer Science student with a strong foundation in programming and a passion for designing and implementing robust software solutions.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

experience.title: AI/ML Research Intern
achievements: Implemented code for lung cancer CT screening to enhance the efficiency of pulmonary nodule detection.
Modified and optimized deep learning algorithms in C++ and Python, and extracted patient data for analysis.

experience.title: President, Girls Who Code College Loops
achievements: Spearheaded the establishment of the Girls Who Code College Loop at RPI, organizing events to support female participation in coding activities.
Responsible for overseeing meetings and organizing events to support female participation in tech.

experience.title: Undergraduate Research Assistant (Wild Me Project)
achievements: Utilized YOLOv8 and NumPy for the endangered zebra population in Zambia monitoring via computer vision.
Designed user-friendly web apps for the Wild Me project, integrating machine learning for better data visualization and efficient conservation.

projects.title: Edureka: Event Managing Web Application Team
projects.company: Rensselaer Center for Open Source, RPI, NY
achievements: Designed a mobile application for the RPI President, providing direct access to schedules and bookings on campus.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to build REST APIs in Express.js and MongoDB, enabling seamless event data integration with two other event-scheduling mobile applications.

projects.title: HackRPI Mobile Application Team
projects.company: Rensselaer Center for Open Source, RPI, NY
achievements: Contributed to the front-end development of a mobile app for the annual RPI Hackathon, streamlining participant registration and providing clear access to hackathon statistics
Implemented new features using Figma and React, and managed version control via GitHub repositories.
Developed and tested the app on both Android and iOS platforms using Expo.

conferences: Grace Hopper Celebration for Women in Computing 2023 & 2024
Girls Who Code Hiring Winter Summit
clubsAndOrganizations: Member of Girls Who Code Association at RPI
